title: Syracuse Common Council wants residents’ opinions on using drones for police calls
summary: Syracuse Common Council unanimously approved a public hearing (Sept. 15) on the Syracuse Police Department's proposal to deploy unmanned drones as first responders to 911 calls. The council is seeking community input on privacy and data-sharing concerns before voting on a $57,000 five-year licensing contract with Axon Enterprises for FAA remote operation clearance. The city previously approved $250,000 for drone equipment in November.

political_signal.description: Council taking cautious approach with mandatory public hearing before contract approval; prior working group feedback was 'mostly opposed' despite eventual approval, indicating ongoing community concern and council responsiveness to resident input.

processing_notes: Dollar amount $99 appears to be unrelated sponsored content (hearing aids ad); excluded from financial entities.
Article contains embedded sponsored content and unrelated article links; core content is clean.
Public hearing date (Sept. 15, 2025) is future-scheduled action; contract vote pending.
